question_text: Did you pay eligible tuition fees in 2024?

ui_hints.help_text: Eligible tuition is usually shown on your T2202 form and must generally be at least $100 per institution.

question_text: Roughly how much did you pay in eligible tuition?

ui_hints.help_text: The federal tuition credit is usually estimated as 15% of eligible tuition.

question_text: Did your school give you a T2202, or a TL11 form if studying abroad?

ui_hints.help_text: Canadian schools usually issue T2202 slips by the end of February through the student portal.

question_text: Did you study at an institution outside Canada in 2024?

ui_hints.help_text: This matters for tuition eligibility rules, not whether you count as a student.

question_text: Did you have a refund or owe taxes in 2024? If you are not sure, did you have employment income or significant taxable scholarship income?

options.label: Not sure, and I had employment or significant taxable scholarship income
Not sure, and I had little or no income

ui_hints.help_text: A refund does not always mean you had no tax payable. Check Schedule 11 or your Notice of Assessment to confirm whether tuition credits were used.

question_text: Did you pay interest on a government student loan in 2024, or in any of the past 5 years?

ui_hints.help_text: Interest on private bank lines of credit or personal loans does not qualify.

question_text: Roughly how much eligible student loan interest did you pay? If unsure, leave this blank and we will point you to your NSLSC interest statement.
Did you move at least 40km closer to your school in 2024?

ui_hints.help_text: Students may deduct moving expenses only if the move was at least 40km closer and other income rules are met.

question_text: At your new school location, did you have any of the following?

options.label: Taxable scholarship, bursary, fellowship, or research grant income
Part-time job, co-op, or self-employment income

ui_hints.help_text: This is the common gotcha: moving expenses can only be deducted against eligible income connected to the new location.

question_text: Roughly how much employment or self-employment income did you earn at the new school location?

ui_hints.help_text: Moving expense deductions are capped by eligible income at the new location.

question_text: Will you be 19 or older by April 2026?

ui_hints.help_text: People turning 19 before April 2026 should file a 2024 return to be considered for the GST/HST credit.

question_text: Are you a resident of Canada for tax purposes?

ui_hints.help_text: Residency for tax purposes affects benefit eligibility. If unsure, CRA Form NR74 can help you determine status.

question_text: Have you accumulated a Canada Training Credit limit on your past CRA Notice of Assessment?

ui_hints.help_text: This is usually relevant for mature students age 26 to under 66. Check your latest CRA Notice of Assessment for your CTCL.

question_text: What province or territory did you live in on December 31, 2024?

ui_hints.help_text: Province of residence on December 31 determines which provincial credits apply.
